title: pimelandra semidentata
side_effects: Pimelandra semidentata can have side effects if used improperly, such as vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain due to its toxic compounds. Ingesting the plant can also lead to liver and kidney damage, as well as an increased risk of bleeding and bruising. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can cause skin irritation, including redness, itching, and rashes. In severe cases, improper use of Pimelandra semidentata can result in respiratory failure and even death.
